2026-04-09T03:12:58.9451823Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m

2026-04-09T03:12:58.9452509Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

2026-04-09T03:15:35.7418705Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T03:14:04.246966887Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57972,\"title\":\"[AI Agents Persistent] Docs: Document TypeSpec emitter migration changes in CHANGELOG and README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57631) by `@radhgupta`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57631 migrated `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` to a new TypeSpec emitter. This introduced:\\n- A `BackwardCompat/` folder with five new shim files maintaining API compatibility\\n- `metadata` parameter type changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on client methods across five classes\\n- Multiple enum-like types internalized or removed by the new emitter, now provided as backward-compat stubs\\n- Extension builder class renamed from `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` → `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`\\n- 970 API additions and 145 deletions in the public surface\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section is completely empty** — the emitter migration and all resulting API surface changes are not recorded anywhere\\n- **Breaking change not documented**: `metadata` parameter type changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on `CreateAgent`, `UpdateAgent`, `CreateMessage`, `UpdateMessage`, `UpdateRun`, `CreateThread`, `UpdateThread`, `CreateVectorStore`, `ModifyVectorStore` (and their async counterparts)\\n- **Type internalization not documented**: The following types were removed from generated code and are now preserved only as backward-compat stubs — users relying on them directly need to know: `AzureFunctionBindingType`, `FileSearchToolCallContentType`, `MessageDeltaChunkObject`, `PersistentAgentsVectorStoreObject`, `ResponseFormatJsonSchemaTypeType`, `RunStepDeltaChunkObject`, `VectorStoreFileBatchObject`, `VectorStoreFileObject`\\n- **Extension builder rename not documented**: `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` renamed to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` — old name preserved as a stub but callers referencing the new name need guidance\\n- **README missing migration note**: No section or callout explains the emitter migration, backward-compat strategy, or how existing users may be affected when upgrading to `1.2.0-beta.11`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit — populate the `1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n- The `metadata` parameter type has changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on the following methods (and their async counterparts):\\n  - `PersistentAgentsAdministrationClient.CreateAgent` / `UpdateAgent`\\n  - `ThreadMessages.CreateMessage` / `UpdateMessage`\\n  - `ThreadRuns.UpdateRun`\\n  - `Threads.CreateThread` / `UpdateThread`\\n  - `VectorStores.CreateVectorStore` / `ModifyVectorStore`\\n  \\n  Backward-compatible overloads accepting `IReadOnlyDictionary` are provided and marked `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]`. Callers passing `Dictionary` or `IReadOnlyDictionary` will continue to compile without changes.\\n\\n- The DI extension class `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` (in `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure`) was renamed to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`. The old name is preserved as a backward-compatible stub.\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n\\n- Migrated code generation to the new TypeSpec emitter. Several internal enum-like types (`AzureFunctionBindingType`, `FileSearchToolCallContentType`, `MessageDeltaChunkObject`, `PersistentAgentsVectorStoreObject`, `ResponseFormatJsonSchemaTypeType`, `RunStepDeltaChunkObject`, `VectorStoreFileBatchObject`, `VectorStoreFileObject`) that were previously generated are now maintained as backward-compatible stubs to preserve API compatibility.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/README.md`\\n\\nEdit — add a **Migration notice** callout near the top of the `## Key concepts` section (after line 67, before the \\u0026#34;### Create and authenticate the client\\u0026#34; subsection):\\n\\n```markdown\\n\\u0026gt; [!NOTE]\\n\\u0026gt; **1.2.0-beta.11 emitter migration**: This release was regenerated with a new TypeSpec emitter. The `metadata` parameter type changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on several client methods. Backward-compatible overloads are provided so most callers will compile without changes. If you see overload-resolution warnings or errors after upgrading, switch your `metadata` argument from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `Dictionary`.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for these changes — the CHANGELOG and README prose edits above are sufficient.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe existing README contains all required sections in the correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nNo structural changes are needed; only the migration note addition described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 1: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit — populate the `1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n- The `metadata` parameter type has changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on the following methods (and their async counterparts):\\n  - `PersistentAgentsAdministrationClient.CreateAgent` / `UpdateAgent`\\n  - `ThreadMessages.CreateMessage` / `UpdateMessage`\\n  - `ThreadRuns.UpdateRun`\\n  - `Threads.CreateThread` / `UpdateThread`\\n  - `VectorStores.CreateVectorStore` / `ModifyVectorStore`\\n  \\n  Backward-compatible overloads accepting `IReadOnlyDictionary` are provided and marked `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]`. Callers passing `Dictionary` or `IReadOnlyDictionary` will continue to compile without changes.\\n\\n- The DI extension class `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` (in `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure`) was renamed to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`. The old name is preserved as a backward-compatible stub.\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n\\n- Migrated code generation to the new TypeSpec emitter. Several internal enum-like types (`AzureFunctionBindingType`, `FileSearchToolCallContentType`, `MessageDeltaChunkObject`, `PersistentAgentsVectorStoreObject`, `ResponseFormatJsonSchemaTypeType`, `RunStepDeltaChunkObject`, `VectorStoreFileBatchObject`, `VectorStoreFileObject`) that were previously generated are now maintained as backward-compatible stubs to preserve API compatibility.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/README.md`\\n\\nEdit — add a **Migration notice** callout near the top of the `## Key concepts` section (after line 67, before the \\u0026#34;### Create and authenticate the client\\u0026#34; subsection):\\n\\n```markdown\\n\\u0026gt; [!NOTE]\\n\\u0026gt; **1.2.0-beta.11 emitter migration**: This release was regenerated with a new TypeSpec emitter. The `metadata` parameter type changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` on several client methods. Backward-compatible overloads are provided so most callers will compile without changes. If you see overload-resolution warnings or errors after upgrading, switch your `metadata` argument from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `Dictionary`.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for these changes — the CHANGELOG and README prose edits above are sufficient.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe existing README contains all required sections in the correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nNo structural changes are needed; only the migration note addition described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-09T03:15:35.7594405Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T03:14:13.615504927Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":24,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute streamingUpdate in responseStream) // this throws\\n        {\\n            // we never get here\\n        }\\n\\n        if (toolOutputs.Count \\u003e 0)\\n        {\\n            responseStream =\\n                _persistentAgentsClient.Runs.SubmitToolOutputsToStreamAsync(run, toolOutputs, cancellationToken);\\n            continue;\\n        }\\n\\n        break;\\n    } while (true);\\n}\\n```\\n\\nI can not wrap it inside a try catch since inside th foreach I am yielding individual parts back over the API:\\n```csharp\\n// `processUserMessageAsync` is the
